old_claim.note: Cadastral Maps provides a capability that enables this technology without being the only possible path.

new_claim.note: State cadastral surveys are administrative programs built around parcel/property maps, so surveyed cadastral mapping is the direct artifact prerequisite.

source_shape.source_locator: Kain and Baigent trace cadastral maps as state property-mapping instruments and describe early modern cadastral survey for taxation in the northern Netherlands from a 1533 order.
source_shape.source_claim_summary: The source treats cadastral maps as property-mapping instruments used by states for land and tax administration.
source_shape.source_support_rationale: The edge is required because this node is scoped to state cadastral survey programs, not to surveying theory in general.

ontology_before: State Cadastral Surveys treated Cadastral Maps as one enabling capability among several mapping and tax-administration prerequisites.
ontology_after: State Cadastral Surveys treats surveyed cadastral maps as the required artifact class around which the state program is organized.
invariant_preserved_or_changed: Changed: surveyed_cadastral_maps is tightened from enabling to required.
would_reject_if: The node is broadened to non-map written land inventories only.
A stronger source shows the 1533 program used no cadastral maps or parcel/property survey.
surveyed_cadastral_maps is split into a narrower early_modern_cadastral_maps node that should replace this prerequisite.
short_reason: Cadastral state surveys require cadastral maps.
